content.text: OperatorSymbolFormOperationComma,x, yEvaluate x then y, returns value of y
OperatorSymbolFormOperationComma,x, yEvaluate x then y, returns value of y

content.code:
z = (a, b); // evaluate (a, b) first to get result of b, then assign that value to variable z.
z = a, b; // evaluates as "(z = a), b", so z gets assigned the value of a, and b is evaluated and discarded.

content.text: Best practiceAvoid using the comma operator, except withinfor loops.
Avoid using the comma operator, except withinfor loops.

There is no need to avoid separator commas (except when declaring multiple variables, which you should not do).Next lesson6.6The conditional operatorBack to table of contentsPrevious lesson6.4Increment/decrement operators, and side effects
